options.name: Greek yoghurt bowl
options.notes: Full-fat Greek yoghurt, mixed berries, handful of granola, drizzle of honey. Stir in creatine.

options.name: Scrambled eggs with smoked salmon
options.notes: High protein, virtually zero carb. Fast — 8 minutes. Good on very low calorie days.

options.name: Greek yoghurt with berries (deficit)
options.notes: No-cook, 2 minutes. Add creatine stirred in. No honey on deficit days.

options.name: Scrambled eggs on sourdough
options.notes: 3 eggs scrambled in butter, 2 slices sourdough, smoked salmon optional. 10 minutes.

options.name: Protein oats
options.notes: Oats cooked with milk, scoop of unflavoured whey or Greek yoghurt stirred in, banana, peanut butter. Prep night before for overnight oats.

options.name: Tamago gohan (egg on rice)
options.notes: Hot Japanese short-grain rice, 2 raw eggs, soy sauce, sesame oil, furikake. Classic Japanese breakfast — 5 minutes if rice is precooked.

options.name: Smoked salmon bagel
options.notes: Wholegrain bagel, cream cheese, smoked salmon, capers, red onion. Good prep-free option.

options.name: Full English (lighter version)
options.notes: 2 eggs, 2 bacon medallions, grilled tomato, mushrooms, 1 slice sourdough. Weekend option — 20 minutes.

options.name: Protein porridge
options.notes: Oats cooked with water, vanilla whey stirred in once off heat, berries on top. Stir creatine in too. 5 minutes.

options.name: Cottage cheese with berries and almonds
options.notes: No-cook, 1 minute. Almonds add healthy fat and a bit of crunch — don't skip them, they make the texture work.

options.name: Egg white and cottage cheese scramble with smoked salmon
options.notes: Designed for the macro plan — very high protein-to-calorie ratio. Scramble whites and whole egg together, fold in cottage cheese and smoked salmon off the heat so the cottage cheese stays creamy rather than splitting.

options.name: Overnight oats — vanilla berry
options.notes: Prep the night before — combine everything in a jar, stir, fridge overnight. Ready to eat cold, no morning effort at all.

options.name: Overnight oats — chocolate peanut butter
options.notes: Unflavoured protein here — vanilla clashes with cocoa. Stir peanut butter through while still slightly warm from fridge for easier mixing, or melt 5 seconds in microwave.

options.name: Overnight oats — apple cinnamon
options.notes: Grate the apple skin-on for extra fibre — it softens completely overnight. Vanilla protein works well here, cinnamon ties it together.

options.name: Overnight oats — banana
options.notes: Unflavoured protein lets the banana carry the sweetness without it tasting artificial. Mash half the banana into the mix and slice the rest on top before eating.

ingredients.name: banana, mashed (other half sliced on top)

options.name: Ham and egg white scramble with light cheddar
options.notes: Designed for the macro plan. Scramble whites with the whole egg, fold in diced ham and grated cheddar at the end so the cheese melts without overcooking the eggs.

options.name: Protein pancakes
options.notes: Blend oats, egg whites, whey and a splash of water into a batter. Fry small pancakes in a dry non-stick pan, 1-2 minutes per side. A few berries on top if carb budget allows.

options.name: Turkey bacon and eggs with cherry tomatoes
options.notes: Classic combination — grill the turkey bacon while scrambling the eggs and egg white together. Tomatoes halved and seared in the same pan for a minute.

options.name: Greek yoghurt protein bowl with almonds
options.notes: No-cook, 2 minutes. Stir the whey into the yoghurt until fully dissolved before adding almonds — whisking briefly avoids any powdery texture.

options.name: Cottage cheese, turkey and boiled egg bowl
options.notes: No-cook if turkey and egg are prepped ahead. Boil the egg the night before for a genuinely 2-minute breakfast.

options.name: High-protein chia pudding
options.notes: Whisk whey into water until smooth before adding chia — prevents clumping. Stir again after 5 minutes, then fridge overnight or eat once thickened (20+ min).

options.name: Smoked salmon and three-egg-white scramble with cucumber
options.notes: Scramble the egg whites and whole egg gently, fold in flaked smoked salmon off the heat so it just warms through. Cucumber slices on the side.

options.name: Chicken sausage, egg white and spinach scramble with parmesan
options.notes: Slice and brown the chicken sausages first, add spinach to wilt, then pour in whisked egg whites and stir through. Grate parmesan over at the end.

options.name: Tofu and turkey bacon scramble
options.notes: Crumble firm tofu into a hot pan with turmeric for colour, add diced turkey bacon, fold in whisked egg whites at the end. Good dairy-free option.

options.name: Cottage cheese pancakes
options.notes: Blend cottage cheese, egg, egg whites and oats until mostly smooth. Fry small pancakes in a dry non-stick pan, 2 minutes per side. Texture is denser than regular pancakes.

options.name: Chicken and quinoa bowl
options.notes: Grilled chicken breast, quinoa, roasted veg, olive oil, lemon. Batch cook quinoa on Sunday.

options.name: Half omelette with mackerel, cheddar and spinach
options.notes: Make a 4-egg omelette, eat half. Pair with 150g Greek yoghurt to hit protein target. Save second half for tomorrow.

options.name: Tuna and cottage cheese bowl
options.notes: Highest protein lunch per calorie on the plan. Tuna, cottage cheese, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, lemon juice, black pepper. No cooking.

options.name: Mackerel on rye with spinach salad
options.notes: Tinned mackerel, 2 rye crispbreads, large spinach salad with cherry tomatoes and lemon dressing. Fast and portable.

options.name: Miso soup with tofu and rice
options.notes: Dashi-based miso soup, silken tofu, wakame, spring onion, steamed rice. Keep dashi stock in fridge. 10 minutes.

options.name: Tuna nicoise salad
options.notes: Tinned tuna or seared, boiled eggs, green beans, olives, cherry tomatoes, Dijon dressing. No cooking needed if using tinned.

options.name: Lentil and vegetable soup
options.notes: Red lentils, tomatoes, cumin, coriander, spinach. Batch cook Sunday — reheats in 3 minutes. Add crusty bread for extra kcal.

options.name: Soba noodle salad
options.notes: Cold soba, edamame, cucumber, spring onion, sesame seeds, soy-ginger dressing. Good cold — works as desk lunch.

options.name: Masoor dal soup with naan
options.notes: Red lentil dal spiced with cumin, turmeric and coriander, finished with a tarka of ghee and mustard seeds. Mini naan on the side. Batch cook — reheats in 3 minutes.

options.name: Dosa with sambar and coconut chutney
options.notes: Use ready-made dosa batter (Indian supermarket or online) for weekday speed. Sambar paste is fine. Coconut chutney: desiccated coconut, ginger, lemon. 20 minutes with batter ready.

options.name: Black bean and corn quesadillas
options.notes: Black beans, sweetcorn, cheddar, smoked paprika, cumin, wholegrain tortillas. Serve with soured cream and salsa. 15 minutes — good desk lunch cut into wedges.

options.name: Chicken and avocado wrap
options.notes: Wholegrain wrap, grilled chicken, avocado, spinach, Greek yoghurt dressing. Quick to assemble if chicken is pre-cooked.

options.name: Half omelette with chicken, cheddar and spinach (no mackerel)
options.notes: Variant of the mackerel omelette for days without tinned fish. Make a 4-egg omelette, eat half. Diced cooked chicken folded in with the cheese and spinach off the heat.

options.name: Tuna on rye with spinach salad (no mackerel)
options.notes: Variant of the mackerel rye lunch using tuna instead. Same format — fast, portable, minimal prep.

options.name: Prawn and avocado salad
options.notes: No-cook if using pre-cooked prawns. Large bowl — feels substantial despite the calorie count. Lemon and black pepper dressing only.

options.name: Chicken and cottage cheese bowl with cherry tomatoes
options.notes: Very high protein-to-calorie ratio. Slice cooked chicken over cottage cheese, halve the tomatoes on top. No-cook if chicken is pre-cooked.

options.name: Turkey breast and cottage cheese wrap
options.notes: Spread cottage cheese on the wrap instead of mayo or sauce — adds protein with almost no extra fat. Layer turkey and salad, roll tightly.

options.name: Prawn and cottage cheese salad
options.notes: No-cook if using pre-cooked prawns. Toss everything together with lemon — the cottage cheese acts almost like a creamy dressing.

options.name: Beef and bean salad bowl
options.notes: Brown the beef mince with cumin and paprika, cool slightly, toss through kidney beans and salad. Good warm or cold.

options.name: Egg and tuna salad
options.notes: No-cook if eggs are boiled ahead. Flake tuna over chopped boiled eggs and leaves, dress with lemon and black pepper.

options.name: Chicken tikka skewers with raita
options.notes: Marinate chicken in tikka paste and yoghurt, grill or air-fry until charred. Raita: grated cucumber stirred into low-fat yoghurt with mint.

items.name: chicken breast, marinated in tikka paste

options.name: Salmon and edamame salad
options.notes: Pan-fry or bake the salmon, flake over edamame and salad leaves. A small squeeze of lime and a few drops of soy sauce work well.

options.name: Chicken and chickpea salad
options.notes: Toss warm or cold chicken with chickpeas, cucumber and tomato. Lemon, olive oil-free dressing keeps fat low — herbs and lemon do the work.

options.name: Tofu, edamame and egg white poke bowl
options.notes: Pan-fry tofu cubes until golden, scramble the egg whites separately, combine with edamame and cucumber. Soy and rice vinegar to dress.

options.name: Turkey meatballs with tzatziki
options.notes: Form turkey mince into small balls with garlic and oregano, bake or pan-fry. Tzatziki: Greek yoghurt, grated cucumber, garlic, mint.

items.name: turkey breast mince, formed into meatballs

dinner.label: Dinner
dinner.meta: ~650–750 kcal · Mix of quick and proper cook

options.name: Salmon teriyaki with rice and greens
options.notes: Salmon fillet in homemade teriyaki (soy, mirin, sake, sugar), steamed rice, bok choy or tenderstem. 20–25 minutes. Use your mirin and sake.

options.name: Baked chicken breast with tenderstem and rice
options.notes: Simple, high protein, low fat. Season well — garlic powder, paprika, salt. Bake 20 min at 200°C. Weigh rice dry.

options.name: Salmon with roasted veg
options.notes: Salmon is calorie-dense from fat but excellent protein and omega-3. Keep portion tight, skip rice — roasted veg fills the plate. No oil needed.

options.name: Breaded chicken with pickled cabbage, broccoli and rice
options.notes: Your go-to. Weigh the rice cooked. Katsu sauce included. Pickled cabbage is essentially free calories.

options.name: Beef stir-fry with broccoli and noodles
options.notes: Sirloin strips — lean cut keeps calories down. Weigh noodles dry. Minimal oil, high heat. Soy and ginger only — skip oyster sauce on deficit days.

options.name: Chicken thigh katsu curry
options.notes: Panko-crumbed chicken thighs, Japanese curry roux (S&B block), steamed rice, shredded cabbage. 35–40 minutes. A proper cook.

options.name: Beef and vegetable stir-fry
options.notes: Sirloin strips, broccoli, sugar snap peas, oyster sauce, ginger, garlic, rice. 20 minutes. High heat essential.

options.name: Baked cod with roasted vegetables
options.notes: Cod fillet, cherry tomatoes, courgette, peppers, olive oil, lemon, capers. Tray bake — 30 minutes, minimal effort.

options.name: Tofu and aubergine dengaku
options.notes: Grilled aubergine and tofu with white miso glaze (miso, mirin, sugar, sake), steamed rice, sesame. 30 minutes. Vegetarian — good for lighter days.

options.name: Lamb chops with roasted root veg
options.notes: Lamb chops with rosemary and garlic, roasted sweet potato and parsnip, wilted greens. 40 minutes — proper weekend cook.

options.name: Prawn and vegetable ramen
options.notes: Dashi or chicken broth, ramen noodles, prawns, soft-boiled egg, corn, spring onion, nori. 25 minutes. Keep dashi in fridge.

options.name: Chicken and chickpea one-pot
options.notes: Chicken thighs, chickpeas, tomatoes, spinach, smoked paprika, cumin. One pan — 35 minutes. Excellent batch cook.

options.name: Beef ragu with pappardelle
options.notes: Slow-cooked beef shin or chuck, San Marzano tomatoes, red wine, soffritto, pappardelle, parmesan. 3–4 hrs low and slow — batch cook Sunday, reheats brilliantly all week.

options.name: Spaghetti carbonara
options.notes: Guanciale or pancetta, eggs, pecorino, black pepper. No cream — ever. 20 minutes. The technique is everything: off the heat before adding eggs.

options.name: Chicken tikka masala
options.notes: Tikka paste-marinated chicken thighs, charred under grill, simmered in tomato and cream sauce. Basmati rice. 35 minutes — paste does the heavy lifting.

options.name: Saag aloo
options.notes: Waxy potatoes and spinach in a spiced tomato and onion masala. Cumin, mustard seeds, turmeric, garam masala. Basmati rice or naan. 30 minutes. Vegetarian — good lighter option.

options.name: Dal makhani
options.notes: Black lentils and kidney beans slow-cooked with tomato, butter and cream. Better the next day — ideal batch cook. Basmati rice or naan. 45 minutes active, ideally 2+ hrs total.

options.name: Kerala prawn curry
options.notes: Prawns in coconut milk and tomato with curry leaves, mustard seeds, and mild green chilli. South Indian — lighter than North Indian curries. Steamed rice. 25 minutes.

options.name: Lamb rogan josh
options.notes: Shoulder of lamb slow-braised in rogan josh paste with whole spices and yoghurt. Rich, aromatic, mild heat. Basmati rice. Paste for weeknight — scratch on weekends.

options.name: Chilli con carne with rice
options.notes: Beef mince, kidney beans, chipotle, cumin, smoked paprika, dark chocolate. Steamed rice, soured cream, coriander. Batch cook — better on day two. 45 minutes.

options.name: Prawn and vegetable stir-fry with noodles
options.notes: Very high protein for the calorie count. King prawns, mixed veg, light soy and sesame. High heat, quick cook — don't overcook the prawns or they turn rubbery.

options.name: Turkey mince chilli with rice
options.notes: Leaner alternative to beef chilli — turkey breast mince keeps fat very low while protein stays high. Same method as the beef version. Batch cooks well.

options.name: Baked cod with new potatoes and green beans
options.notes: Light, clean dinner — good for a lower-effort evening. Cod is very lean so protein-to-calorie ratio is excellent. Roast potatoes in their skins with a little oil.

options.name: Beef fillet with sweet potato mash and asparagus
options.notes: Fillet is the leanest premium steak cut — sear hard, rest well. Mash the sweet potato with nothing added to keep it simple.

options.name: Chicken fajita bowl with rice
options.notes: Slice chicken and peppers, fry hard and fast with fajita spices. Serve over rice — no tortilla keeps the carbs in check.

options.name: Cod and chickpea stew
options.notes: Simmer chickpeas in a light tomato sauce with garlic and paprika, nestle cod fillet in to poach for the last 8 minutes. One pot.

options.name: Turkey burgers with sweet potato wedges
options.notes: Form turkey mince into patties with a little garlic and herbs, grill or pan-fry. Sweet potato wedges roasted at 200°C for 25 minutes.

items.name: turkey breast mince, formed into patties

options.name: Prawn and chickpea curry with rice
options.notes: Light coconut curry — chickpeas and prawns in a tomato-coconut base with mild spices. Prawns go in last, 2-3 minutes only.

options.name: Pork tenderloin with roasted vegetables
options.notes: Pork tenderloin is very lean and high protein. Roast whole at 200°C for 20-25 minutes alongside the vegetables, rest before slicing.

options.name: Chicken and red lentil dal
options.notes: Cook red lentils with onion, garlic, cumin and turmeric until soft. Stir in diced cooked chicken at the end to warm through. Very high protein for the calorie count.

options.name: Beef and broccoli rice bowl
options.notes: Simpler than a stir-fry — sear beef strips hard, steam broccoli separately, serve both over rice with a light soy drizzle.

options.name: Baked salmon with quinoa and green beans
options.notes: Bake salmon at 200°C for 12-15 minutes. Quinoa cooked in stock for extra flavour, green beans steamed or blanched.

options.name: Chicken and black bean burrito bowl
options.notes: Season and grill chicken, serve over black beans and a small portion of rice. Salsa and lime instead of cheese or soured cream keeps it lean.
